data.title: Warner Bros Discovery reported Q2 2025 earnings, aiming to achieve $2.4B adjusted EBITDA for studios, 150M+ Max subscribers by 2026.
data.description: Warner Bros. Discovery Inc (NASDAQ: WBD) reported its Q2 2025 financial results, highlighting a focus on creative talent, film/TV production, and streaming services. The company reported strong momentum in various areas, including opening five consecutive films with over $45 million in domestic box office, leading in Emmy nominations, and attracting subscribers to Max. They have invested in their studios' creative capabilities, aiming for $2.4 billion adjusted EBITDA in 2025. The streaming business is on track to exceed $1.3 billion adjusted EBITDA in 2025 and reach over 150 million subscribers by 2026. The balance sheet has improved, with net leverage at 3.3 times. The company plans to split into two independent publicly traded companies in 2026. Content licensing strategies and potential future franchises, like Harry Potter and Superman, were discussed during the earnings call. Gunnar Wiedenfels, the CFO, highlighted the shift in content mix and the importance of sports rights. The underappreciated growth potential for Global Networks was also noted.

data.title: Core Scientific (CORZ) will release earnings on 2025-08-08, with analysts expecting an EPS of $-0.02. Investors hope for positive news.
data.description: Core Scientific (NASDAQ: CORZ) is set to unveil its quarterly earnings on 08/08/2025. Analysts predict an earnings per share (EPS) of -$0.02. Investors are eager for potential outperformance against forecasts and optimistic guidance for the subsequent quarter. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Shopify outperformed in Q2, with sales rising; analysts raise price targets: Boone to $185, Smith to $179, Berg maintains $180.
data.description: Shopify Inc (NASDAQ: SHOP) has seen positive second-quarter sales, leading to at least two analysts adjusting their price targets. Andrew Boone of JMP Securities reaffirmed a Market Outperform rating with a new price target of $185, up from $135. Reginald Smith from JPMorgan also reiterated an Overweight rating and increased the price target from $115 to $179. Needham's analyst Scott Berg kept a Buy rating with a price target of $180. According to JMP Securities, Shopify's revenue and GMV growth, excluding foreign exchange impacts, are accelerating at 30% and 29% year-on-year respectively. The company is reportedly gaining market share in enterprise accounts, with a positive macroeconomic outlook. Analysts highlight Shopify's strengthening competitive position through the addition of new features like checkout kit for AI agents and addressing gaps in the product portfolio like multi-entity support. More details are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TeraWulf (WULF) to announce quarterly earnings: analysts project EPS of $-0.06. Previous misses led to price drops. Stock trading at $5.15, up 33.51% in past year. Analysts rate WULF a Buy with $5.67 target price (10.1% potential increase).
data.description: TeraWulf is set to release its quarterly earnings soon with analysts expecting an EPS of $-0.06. The company will aim to beat this estimate and provide positive guidance for the next quarter. Historical data shows a past EPS miss resulting in a 0.0% drop in stock price. TeraWulf's stock was at $5.15 on August 06, up 33.51% over the last 52 weeks. Analysts suggest a Buy rating with a $5.67 one-year price target, reflecting a potential 10.1% increase. Investors should focus on the company's guidance alongside the earnings result for potential stock price movement.

data.title: Paxos fined $26.5M for Binance USD due diligence failings in partnership with Binance.
data.description: Paxos collaborated with Binance to launch Binance USD, but the New York financial regulator stated that inadequate due diligence was conducted in the process.

data.description: A recent report reveals that the annual carbon emissions from the aviation industry have reached a staggering 915 million tons. This number represents a 32% increase compared to the levels recorded in 2013. This surge is primarily due to the growing demand for air travel worldwide. The report also highlights that the industry's emissions are on track to exceed 1 billion tons by 2025 if current trends persist. Additionally, it states that the aviation sector is responsible for approximately 2.5% of global carbon dioxide emissions, with this figure expected to rise in the coming years. The data serves as a stark reminder of the environmental impact of air travel and underscores the urgent need for sustainable solutions to mitigate these emissions.

data.title: Money Management International partners with CO's Office of Just Transition to support coal workers & families with free financial counseling & services as they prepare for industry transition.anon support for closure of coal facilities, offering confidential financial coaching, debt reduction,credit improvement,& ongoing support to eligible workers.
data.description: Money Management International (MMI) has partnered with the Colorado Office of Just Transition (OJT) to provide financial counseling to Colorado workers impacted by the transition away from coal-based energy production. The partnership aims to support coal workers and their families as they prepare for the closure of coal transition facilities. Eligible workers will receive free financial counseling services including personalized coaching, debt reduction, credit improvement, and ongoing support paid for by OJT. The program is designed to assist Colorado residents directly employed in coal-based facilities, offering essential financial guidance during career transitions or retirement. The partnership seeks to empower workers to plan their next steps as Colorado focuses on supporting coal communities across the state.
